channel.title: analysis on Alan Aw
channel.description: Recent content in analysis on Alan Aw

item.title: Cleaning and Visualizing 1000 Genomes Data
item.description: In 2008, scientists launched the 1000 Genomes Project to catalogue human genetic variation across the globe. This \(N=2504\) by \(P=84.4 \times 10^6\) dataset (in Phase 3) has spurred the development of models and algorithms to relate our genome to many downstream measurements (like gene expression, transcriptome, epigenome, proteome and phenotypes), as well as to upstream biological processes like human population demography and history. If there is one thing that hinders my progress at analyzing real biological datasets, it is bioinformatics.

item.title: Lagrange Multipliers Revisited
item.description: Happy New Year! This is my first post, and I intend to use this space to write about my thoughts and learning. Any constructive feedback would be appreciated! Now for the actual post. I’ve been thinking about mathematics over Winter break, mostly because I often find myself overlooking details about important results and consequently learning maths not as thoroughly as I’d like. I find this especially true with regard to analysis (or continuous maths in general), so I try to revisit the maths every now and then.
